ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-07-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP 430.10(E).) PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT THE FIRST THROUGH SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM CONDUCT OTHER THAN DEFENDANT FAILING TO FILE A WORKER'S COMPENSATION CLAIM. PLAINTIFF IS FURTHER GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES ARE A "DISABILITY," AS DEFINED BY FEHA. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT KNEW ABOUT PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY AND DID NOT RESPOND TO REQUESTS FOR ACCOMMODATION. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND THE HARASSMENT CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT'S "ACTIONS [WERE] OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF JOB DUTIES WHICH ARE NOT OF A TYPE NECESSARY TO BUSINESS AND PERSONNEL MANAGEMENT." (RENO V. BAIRD (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640, 647.) LASTLY, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THE CAUSATION ELEMENT OF THE RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION. (YANOWITZ V. L'OREAL USA, INC. (2005) 36 CAL.4TH 1028, 1042.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2014 Continued To Sep-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ILONA BRUSIL, TEL. NO. 775-1793 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately alleges that Plaintiff's injuries limited major life activities and therefore fall under the FEHA definition of "disability." (SAC, pars. 20-22; Gov. Code sec. 12926.) The SAC adequately alleges that Defendant was aware of Plaintiff's disability, but Defendant took no affirmative steps to accommodate him. (SAC, par. 21.) Cal. Code of Regulations, Tit. 2 sec. 11068 provides that an employer has an affirmative duty to make reasonable accommodations for an employee's disability. The SAC adequately alleges that the harassment went beyond the scope of conduct necessary for job performance. (SAC, pars. 23-25, 51.) The SAC adequately alleges the causation element of the retaliation cause of action. (SAC, par. 27.) The SAC adequately states a cause of action for violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 et seq. (See Herr v. Nestle USA, Inc. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 779, 789.) The claims remaining in the SAC are not preempted by the Worker's Compensation Act. Disability discrimination claims and wrongful discharge claims arising from disability discrimination are not preempted. (City of Moorpark v. Sup. Ct. (1998) 18 Cal.4th 1143, 1154-55.) Claims based on failure to accommodate a worker's disability are also not preempted. (Bagatti v. Dept. of Rehabilitation (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 344, 364-68.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/pjb

ATTY. LUIS OROZIO, TEL. NO. 967-3109 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NILS ROSENQUEST, TEL. NO. 292-0980 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege facts establishing that the first through seventh causes of action arise from conduct other than Defendant failing to file a worker's compensation claim. Plaintiff is further granted leave to amend to allege facts establishing Plaintiff's injuries are a "disability," as defined by FEHA. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege facts establishing that Defendant knew about Plaintiff's disability and did not respond to requests for accommodation. Plaintiff is also given leave to amend the harassment cause of action to plead facts establishing that Defendant's "actions [were] outside the scope of job duties which are not of a type necessary to business and personnel management." (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 647.) Lastly, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts establishing the causation element of the retaliation cause of action. (Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1042.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Off Calendar
